channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Lawyers for Sonya Massey's family say $10M settlement 'first step;' await trial, legislative changes
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — A $10 million settlement between county officials in Springfield, Illinois, and the family of Sonya Massey is the “first step in getting full justice” for the Black woman fatally shot in her home last summer by a sheriff's deputy, lawyers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Eastern storm cuts power to tens of thousands as California braces for flooding
item.description: Storms dumped heavy snow and freezing rain on a swath of the eastern U.S. stretching from Kentucky to the nation's capital, causing hundreds of traffic accidents, knocking out power in places and threatening to flood waterways as temperatures began rising Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Coca-Cola ICE raids? Company faces backlash in face of unfounded rumors
item.description: (KRON) -- A significant backlash has emerged against soft drink manufacturer Coca-Cola in the wake of rumors that the company laid off its own immigrant employees and then called the U.S. Immigration and Customs and Enforcement Agency on them. Rumors have circulated on X, Reddit, and Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds singer R. Kelly's convictions, 30-year prison term
item.description: R. Kelly’s racketeering and sex trafficking convictions, along with a 30-year prison sentence, were upheld Wednesday by a federal appeals court that concluded the singer exploited his fame for over a quarter century to sexually abuse girls and young women.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell only Republican to vote against Gabbard
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) on Wednesday voted against confirming former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) to serve as the nation’s top intelligence official, sending a strong message to fellow GOP senators, some of whom privately doubted her qualifications to hold the job.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: When does a heartbeat start? South Carolina Supreme Court again takes up abortion issue
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — With a heartbeat abortion ban solidly in place in South Carolina, lawyers for the state and Planned Parenthood return to the state's highest court Wednesday to argue how restrictive the ban should be.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI says it found 2,400 new JFK assassination records
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The FBI on Tuesday said it discovered 2,400 new records related to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy as federal agencies work to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order last month to release thousands of files.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AP says reporter blocked from White House over ‘Gulf of Mexico’ policy
item.description: The barring of the journalist, the AP said in a statement, was in connection with AP's refusal to "align its editorial standards with President Donald Trump’s executive order renaming the Gulf of Mexico as the Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alabama officer's minor traffic stop leads to discovery of cocaine being trafficked by illegal aliens
item.description: A police officer in Calera, Alabama, uncovered 46 pounds of cocaine being trafficked by two Colombian nationals after conducting a traffic stop for a minor vehicle violation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE scrutiny could 'take handcuffs off' police officers to do their jobs: expert
item.description: President Donald Trump's creation of DOGE could "take the handcuffs off" local law enforcement and provide accountability on spending, a former NYPD lieutenant said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope rebukes Trump administration over immigrant deportations and appears to aim directly at Vance
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis issued a major rebuke Tuesday to the Trump administration’s plans for mass deportations of migrants, warning that the forceful removal of people purely because of their illegal status deprives them of their inherent dignity and “will end badly.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Musk appears at White House defending DOGE's work but acknowledging mistakes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's most powerful adviser, Elon Musk, made a rare public appearance at the White House on Tuesday to defend the swift and extensive cuts he's pushing across the federal government while acknowledging there have been mistakes and will be more.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Court sets March 19 date for first execution in Arizona in over 2 years
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The Arizona Supreme Court set a March 19 execution date Tuesday for a man who pleaded guilty to murder more than 17 years ago and recently said his death sentence was “long overdue.” It would be the state’s first application of the death penalty in more than two years.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In rare case, Georgia father jailed after his son was accused of a school shooting is granted bond
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — In a rare case of a parent being charged after a school shooting, a judge on Tuesday granted a $500,000 bond for the father of a 14-year-old boy accused of a deadly attack at a north Georgia high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Storm makes for treacherous travel as snow and ice track through the mid-Atlantic
item.description: A snowstorm blew into the mid-Atlantic states Tuesday, causing dozens of accidents on icy roads, prompting school closures and stoking worries about possible power outages. The heaviest snowfall — up to 10 inches (25 centimeters) — was expected in parts of Virginia and West Virginia.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Flu activity now highest in 15 years: See which states have it worst
item.description: Influenza cases continue to mount with the virus reaching activity levels in the U.S. not seen for the last 15 years, according to the Centers from Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: With public broadcasters pressured by the Trump administration, PBS shuts down its diversity office
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — PBS says it is shutting down its office of diversity, equity and inclusion to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order, firing the two executives brought on when the effort was begun in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration appeals Maryland judge's ruling blocking birthright citizenship order
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration on Tuesday said it's appealing a Maryland federal judge's ruling blocking the president's executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship for people whose parents are not legally in the country.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs rattle small business owners already dealing with tight margins
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's continued roll out of a wide array of tariffs is rattling small business owners already dealing with tight profit margins. Trump on Monday announced a 25% tariff on steel and aluminum and promised more import duties to come.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Republicans vow action to boost border funds after briefing with Trump officials
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Citing warnings from President Donald Trump's border czar, Senate Republicans vowed Tuesday to move quickly on a budget plan that could pave the way for the passage of about $340 billion in additional border security and defense spending.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Papa John's debuts bath bomb inspired by its signature garlic sauce
item.description: Papa John’s is giving its fans a new way to enjoy its signature garlic sauce this Valentine’s Day. On Tuesday, the pizza chain announced that they created a limited-edition bath bomb inspired by the famous sauce that comes with every pizza order.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: New York City's mayor has 4 months to persuade Democratic voters he's not Trump's puppet
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams declared Tuesday that he is “no longer facing legal questions” after the Justice Department moved to shield him from the bribery charges that have been hanging over his reelection campaign.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire knocked down, 7 injured in Sun Metro fire
item.description: FINAL UPDATE: Fire knocked down. Crews checking for hot spots. A total of seven patients were assessed- four were transported, and three patients refused. EPFD investigators are on the scene.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Disney, amid changes to DEI initiatives, will alter content warning ahead of 'Peter Pan,' 'Dumbo' and other older films
item.description: Disney has become the latest company to change its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives as pressure from the federal government to eliminate such programs continues.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about the Trump administration moving to drop corruption charges against NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams was thrown a lifeline when the Trump administration moved to drop federal corruption charges against the embattled leader of America’s largest city.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Thousands of tickets issued in Rhode Island over dark window tint
item.description: Massachusetts and Connecticut allow vehicle window tints to be significantly darker than Rhode Island does.
item.dc:creator: Tim White, NewsNation

item.title: Good Samaritan helps deputies capture suspect in foot chase in Denver
item.description: “There was a car on its rims, it didn’t have tires and there was a lot of fire coming from the rims,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Nate Belt, NewsNation

item.title: How photos lost in American disasters find their way home, with a little help from people who care
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hollowed-out homes. Cars entombed by mud. Unpeopled roads. Belongings reduced to dirt and debris. It all took a toll on Taylor Schenker. After Hurricane Helene last September, Schenker was upset by the deluge of images of Asheville, North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: ANNA FURMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Massachusetts top court rules Karen Read can be retried in her boyfriend's death
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The state’s top court ruled Tuesday that Karen Read can be retried on all the same charges in the death of her Boston police officer boyfriend, the latest twist in the long-running case that transfixed true crime fans nationwide.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Roses are red, violets are blue, 940 million flowers are traveling (through Miami) to you
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers. In the run up to Feb. 14, agricultural specialists at Miami International Airport have processed about 940 million stems of cut flowers, according to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Schumer warns of 'Trump shutdown,' lays out 4-pronged plan for Democrats
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) in a letter to colleagues Monday warned of the possibility of a “Trump shutdown” and reminded fellow senators that Democrats have the power to make or break any bill to fund the government past March 14.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: World leaders, CEOs, scientists from over 100 countries are meeting for a high-stakes AI summit
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Major world leaders are meeting for an AI summit in Paris, where challenging diplomatic talks are expected as tech titans fight for dominance in the fast-moving technology industry.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and AAMER MADHANI Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said. The Nome Volunteer Fire Department made the announcement on its Facebook page Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As female representation hits new highs among states, constitutions still assume officials are male
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — South Dakota’s constitution refers to the governor as a “he.” But for the past six years, the governor was a “she” — Kristi Noem, the first female to hold the position there. The conservative Republican pushed a constitutional amendment to change the gendered language.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4
